articles.title: Gemini Live could soon let you toss files into the conversation mid-chat
articles.excerpt: Gemini Live could stop making you describe an image and just take that damn file.
articles.body: Credit: Tushar Mehta / Android Authority TL;DR Google is testing the handy option to attach files to Gemini Live. Currently, Gemini Live is limited to a live video feed from your screen or your surroundings. In addition to adding files from your phone, Gemini Live could allow you to import them directly from Drive. After recently redesigning part of the Gemini launcher on Android, Google is testing similar changes to the assistant’s other aspects, including the floating overlay and Gemini Live . And along with dousing the interface in new “neural” design elements, Google is also experimenting with the option to attach files to your Google Live conversations. You’re reading an Authority Insights story.
articles.author: Tushar Mehta

articles.title: Star Wars will finally air its rarest unreleased show, but only few fans will be able to watch
articles.excerpt: Star Wars Detours, the legendary comedy animated series that was canceled before release, will air selected episodes at the Lucas Museum.
articles.body: Star Wars ' unreleased animated comedy series, Star Wars Detours , has been the object of fans' desire for more than a decade. Produced in 2013 by Lucasfilm Animation in collaboration with Seth Green and Matthew Senreich, the show never aired due to concerns that its parody nature would not be the best introduction to the franchise for new fans. Now, fans finally have a chance to have a look at this gem from the Star Wars vault.
articles.author: Francesco Cacciatore

articles.title: Stop Letting Your Slack Bot Ghost You: Turning Slack Into a Local Terminal for Claude
articles.excerpt: You know the feeling. You call your AI bot in Slack, hit enter, and then... nothing. Dead silence. You sit there staring at the screen. Is it actually running your code? Did the server crash? Is it ju…
articles.body: You know the feeling. You call your AI bot in Slack, hit enter, and then... nothing. Dead silence. You sit there staring at the screen. Is it actually running your code? Did the server crash? Is it just quietly judging your prompt? You have absolutely no idea. It's a black box of anxiety. The standard solution to this UX nightmare isn't much better. Most bots just spam the thread with a million separate status messages: "Claude is thinking..." "Claude is running a command..." "Claude is scratching its nose..." Congratulations, your Slack thread now looks like a chaotic WhatsApp group. We got fed up with both extremes. The "Aha!" Moment: Slack as an IDE While working on the claude-slack-bridge , we realized we didn't just want a chatbot.
articles.author: Tomer Raitz

articles.title: A CSV Viewer That Never Uploads Your Data
articles.excerpt: I just wanted to open a CSV file quickly. Instead, I got: Slow spreadsheet apps Online tools that upload my data Way too much friction So I defined a simple goal: Fast and frictionless Fully local (no…
articles.body: I just wanted to open a CSV file quickly. Instead, I got: Slow spreadsheet apps Online tools that upload my data Way too much friction So I defined a simple goal: Fast and frictionless Fully local (no uploads) Spreadsheet-like experience That’s what I built. What you can do with it Open CSV files instantly (drag & drop, no setup) Search and filter large datasets in seconds Edit data like a spreadsheet Export exactly what you see Why this matters Everything happens locally in your browser: Your data never leaves your device No account required No tracking, no storage Built with client-side JavaScript — no backend involved. Just open, edit, and export. How it works Built with client-side JavaScript — no backend involved.
articles.author: kaylee8520

articles.title: Building RecipeHub: My Experience Developing and Deploying a Modern Recipe Sharing Platform with Django
articles.excerpt: As part of my learning journey with Django, I wanted to build a project that would challenge me beyond the basics. I decided to create RecipeHub, a web application where users can create, manage, and…
articles.body: As part of my learning journey with Django, I wanted to build a project that would challenge me beyond the basics. I decided to create RecipeHub, a web application where users can create, manage, and share recipes while exploring recipes from other users. The project started from a Django starter template, but I customized it by adding new features, redesigning the interface, and deploying it online. Features RecipeHub allows users to: Register and log in Create, edit, and delete recipes Browse recipes by category Save favourite recipes Upload recipe images Access a personal dashboard Use the application in both light and dark mode The application is fully responsive, making it easy to use on both desktop and mobile devices.
articles.author: Hodhan Omar

articles.title: 5 reasons I’d buy the Galaxy Z Fold 8 instead of the Fold 8 Ultra (after using both phones)
articles.excerpt: The cheaper Galaxy Z Fold 8 is the one I'd actually buy.
articles.body: The Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 series is here. And just as everyone expected, Samsung has split its book-style foldable lineup into two models: the Galaxy Z Fold 8 and the Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra. That means, for the first time, you can choose two very different book-style foldables from Samsung. You can either go with the new, wider Galaxy Z Fold 8 or stick with the familiar tall Fold design, now available in its most refined form as the Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra .

articles.title: Spark 4.2 Added Native Vector Search: Do You Still Need a Vector Database?
articles.excerpt: The headline going around is that Spark 4.2 can retire your vector database. That's half true, which is the most dangerous kind of true. Spark did add real vector search, and for some workloads it gen…
articles.body: The headline going around is that Spark 4.2 can retire your vector database. That's half true, which is the most dangerous kind of true. Spark did add real vector search, and for some workloads it genuinely removes a whole system from your stack. For others, you'd regret dropping your vector DB. Here's the honest version. The short answer If your vectors already live in your data platform and your searches are batch or analytical, Spark 4.2 can absolutely replace a separate vector database. If you're serving live, low-latency retrieval for a chatbot or search box, you probably still want a dedicated one. It's a "depends on the workload" answer, and the details matter. What Spark 4.2 actually added Spark 4.2 brought vector search into plain SQL. No bolt-on library, no separate engine.
articles.author: Muskan Bandta

articles.title: Grok 4.5 vs Claude Opus 4.8: Same Code, a Quarter of the Tokens?
articles.excerpt: xAI has a bold pitch for Grok 4.5: it codes about as well as Claude Opus 4.8, but does it with roughly a quarter of the tokens. That's not a "we're smarter" claim. It's a "we're just as good for far l…
articles.body: xAI has a bold pitch for Grok 4.5: it codes about as well as Claude Opus 4.8, but does it with roughly a quarter of the tokens. That's not a "we're smarter" claim. It's a "we're just as good for far less money" claim, which in 2026 might matter more. Someone actually put it to the test, so let me walk through what the numbers say and why you should care. The claim and the pricing Grok 4.5 landed on July 8, 2026. xAI says it matches Opus 4.8 on coding while using about 4.2 times fewer output tokens to get there. The sticker price already favors Grok. It runs $2 per million input tokens and $6 per million output. Opus sits at $5 and $25. That's less than half the price on both sides before you even factor in the token efficiency. Stack the two together and the cost gap gets dramatic.
articles.author: Muskan Bandta

articles.title: The White House Accuses Moonshot of Copying Anthropic: What AI 'Distillation' Actually Means
articles.excerpt: The US government just accused a Chinese AI company of copying an American model, and threatened sanctions over it. Whether or not the claim holds up, it's worth understanding what's actually being al…
articles.body: The US government just accused a Chinese AI company of copying an American model, and threatened sanctions over it. Whether or not the claim holds up, it's worth understanding what's actually being alleged, because the word at the center of it, distillation, is about to come up a lot. Here's the plain version. What distillation means Model distillation is training a new model using the outputs of an existing one. Instead of learning purely from raw data, the new model learns by copying how a stronger "teacher" model responds. Done legitimately, it's a normal technique for making smaller, cheaper models. Done against someone else's commercial model without permission, it's a way to clone a lot of that model's behavior on the cheap, and it usually violates the terms of service.
articles.author: Muskan Bandta

On July 23, during San Diego Comic-Con , publisher Dark Horse announced an upcoming Baldur's Gate 3 comic series.

articles.title: Did Qualcomm’s new Snapdragon Wear Elite chip nuke the Galaxy Watch Ultra 2’s battery gains?
articles.excerpt: Samsung bumped the Galaxy Watch Ultra 2 to an 800mAh battery, but official quotes show no increase in battery life.
articles.body: Credit: Zac Kew-Denniss / Android Authority TL;DR Samsung introduced the Galaxy Watch Ultra 2 with Qualcomm’s Snapdragon Wear Elite chip and a massive 800mAh battery (up 35% from 590mAh), yet official estimates still quote the same “up to 60 hours with Always-On Display” as the original Watch Ultra model. The Galaxy Watch 9 models also increased battery sizes and switched from Exynos W1000 to Snapdragon Wear Elite, but Samsung continues to quote up to 40 hours of runtime without Always-On Display across both generations. The lack of runtime improvement despite Wear OS 7’s efficiency improvements and larger batteries could stem from power draws like the brighter 5,000-nit display, lower processor efficiency, or simply outdated marketing specs.
articles.author: Aamir Siddiqui

articles.title: The $1,200 Z Flip 8 charges slower than a $300 Android phone, and that’s just embarrassing
articles.excerpt: Samsung's Z Flip 8 is outpaced by phones that cost a fraction of its expensive price tag.
articles.body: https://youtu.be/RGTGAkNOjCU Samsung’s flagships have come under fire over the years for lagging behind the best hardware on the market in one way or another. Charging power has been at the heart of many criticisms, historically lagging some way behind the competition. That’s changed somewhat this year. First, the Galaxy S26 Ultra jumped to 60W, and now the Z Fold 8 and 8 Ultra are moving up to 45W as well. Not the fastest in the business, but undoubtedly more in the mix than a couple of years ago.
articles.author: Robert Triggs

articles.title: Ultrahuman’s new ‘Emerald’ update is a massive overhaul of how the app works
articles.excerpt: You can now pair your Apple Watch, Garmin, or WHOOP with the Ultrahuman app.
articles.body: TL;DR Ultrahuman is rolling out a new “Emerald” update for its companion app. It introduces a new “UltraSphere” decision-making engine that suggests “Next Best Actions” to users. It also offers better health insights and new tabs such as “Sleep Screener,” “Longevity,” and “Windows.” Ultrahuman, the company behind fitness rings like the Ultrahuman Ring PRO , today announced a major “Emerald” update to the Ultrahuman app, introducing a fresh design and new features to help users get the most out of their fitness trackers. The update aims to make health insights easier to understand so users can make better health decisions. At the core of the new experience is the “UltraSphere” — a decision engine that lives on the home screen and offers “Next Best Actions” to users.
articles.author: Akshay Gangwar

articles.title: Microsoft talks LG into removing McAfee adware from its… monitors
articles.excerpt: LG's automatically downloaded monitor app will no longer show McAfee pop-ups, but another major concern remains.
articles.body: Credit: Jonathan Feist / Android Authority TL;DR A Microsoft executive has confirmed that LG has agreed to disable McAfee pop-ups on their monitor app. This comes after it emerged that LG was automatically installing bloatware when you connected its monitors to your PC. This doesn’t solve the problem of bloat being installed on your PC without your consent, though. LG came under fire earlier this week after it emerged that it was automatically installing bloatware when you connected its monitors to your PC. What made matters infinitely worse was that the app in question displayed McAfee pop-ups on your desktop whenever you started up your computer. Now, Microsoft executive Pavan Davuluri has announced on X that LG has agreed to disable the McAfee pop-up in their app.
articles.author: Hadlee Simons

articles.title: Gemini’s glowy makeover appears headed for the floating overlay and Live screens
articles.excerpt: Gemini is trying to love more alive than ever.
articles.body: Credit: Adamya Sharma / Android Authority TL;DR Google recently introduced the neural design for its Gemini launcher on Android, and it’s now being tested across other parts, including the floating overlay. These elements include a flowy overlay rather than a solid shape, with colorful dots sprinkled in the background. The redesign also brings new buttons for the Live interface, with a similarly flowy design. Around two months ago, Google introduced Gemini’s new neural design with 3D visual effects and spatial animations , almost gesturing at its transformation for the upcoming Android XR visual interface. The effects were largely limited to Gemini’s homescreen on Android, as well as regular (non-Live) chats.

The Streams chains also need EVAL and XGROUP; the 8.8.0 chain needs EVAL and the bundled RedisBloom module. Redis says the underlying memory flaws may lead to remote code execution. The news comes via an announcement from Green himself during a panel discussion on the museum at San Diego Comic-Con 2026, as reported by Entertainment Weekly. “We made a show with George, an animated series,” said Green. “He wanted to make Simpsons in the Star Wars universe.

articles.title: 'It's Being Written, Is the Short Answer' — Zach Cregger Gives an Update on the Aunt Gladys Prequel | Comic Con 2026
articles.excerpt: Director Zach Cregger said that the Aunt Gladys-focused Weapons prequel movie is in the works and will have "lots of twists and turns and mysteries and crazy things" at San Diego Comic-Con.
articles.body: Weapons and upcoming Resident Evil director Zach Cregger gave an update on the status of his Aunt Gladys prequel film at San Diego Comic-Con on Thursday afternoon. "First of all, I'm really excited about the Gladys movie. I loved working with Amy [Madigan], I love that character," Cregger said. "For the movie Weapons, it really made a lot of sense not to dive into her backstory because the device of Weapons was to keep her very opaque and very mysterious. However, I think there's an amazing story to be told about where Gladys comes from and how she became what we know her as. It's got a lot of twists and turns and mysteries and crazy things, kind of like Weapons does, and I think it's gonna be great.

articles.title: Motor City's director explains how he made the most unique revenge thriller of the year
articles.excerpt: The filmmaker discusses Alan Ritchson, visual storytelling, unforgettable needle drops, and why "the first person to speak usually loses."
articles.body: After spending nearly two decades in development, Motor City has finally reached the big screen. Starring Alan Ritchson as an ex-Army Ranger seeking revenge after being framed and sent to prison, the film tells much of its story with little dialogue, relying instead on physical performances, visual storytelling, and a soundtrack of classic rock needle drops that become as important as the characters themselves.
articles.author: Terry Terrones

articles.title: League of Legends Riftbound TCG: First Impressions of the Vendetta Booster Set | IGN Preview
articles.excerpt: Get an early look at League of Legends Riftbound's upcoming Vendetta expansion.
articles.body: The wait is finally over for Riftbound’s Vendetta booster set, as its release is now just around the corner. This will be the League of Legends TCG’s fourth big drop with pre-release events at local stores starting July 24th with the global release on July 31st. That’s right! With the game having about a year under its belt now, fans finally have a synced-up release with both English and Chinese languages for the first time. Not only is this great for the meta, but players will also have some new deck combinations to mess around with as the set introduces new legends with opposing colors or domains and, of course, new mechanics like Flow to give some extra options out of hand.
articles.author: Mike Mamon

articles.title: Vulcan Centaur swapped out again: NASA goes with SpaceX Falcon Heavy rocket for launch of space weather mission
articles.excerpt: NASA's SunRISE mission has been assigned a new launch vehicle: It will now be delivered to orbit by a SpaceX Falcon Heavy rocket rather than a United Launch Alliance Vulcan Centaur.
articles.body: NASA is shaking things up for its next mission to research the sun. The six satellites of NASA's Sun Radio Interferometer Space Experiment (SunRISE) mission are poised to launch to Earth orbit soon, to serve as an early warning system for incoming solar storms. They were originally slated to lift off on a United Launch Alliance (ULA) Vulcan Centaur rocket but have now been assigned a SpaceX Falcon Heavy , according to a NASA mission update . SunRISE will fly to space as part of a rideshare mission sponsored by the U.S. Space Force's (USSF's) Space Systems Command (SSC). Though it's not stated specifically in the NASA release, that may be a hint as to why the mission's rocket was changed.
articles.author: Josh Dinner

articles.title: 'Star Trek: Strange New Worlds' Season 4: Release date & how to watch Paramount+’s sensational sci-fi series
articles.excerpt: 'We've seen a lot of strange new worlds, but we’ll never see something like this again'
articles.body: 2026 is the 60th anniversary of "Star Trek". To help celebrate the show that creator Gene Roddenberry described as "Wagon Train to the Stars,""Star Trek: Strange New Worlds" returns to Paramount+ for its penultimate season. From teasers and trailers that first dropped last summer and fall, we’re anticipating a rollicking batch of dynamic episodes that draw on diverse subgenres, with everything from classic film noir crime stories and time travel to spooky ghost stories and sword-clashing swashbucklers. But the end is nigh for this colorful modern interpretation of "Trek". Next year's truncated fifth season will be the crew's final mission, so let's revel in the time we have left and beam onto the Enterprise bridge for a promising round of missions.
